name: Coy, Albergue Coto Real – Ruta Circular – Sierra de Burete
region_intro: Mountain biking around Lorca offers diverse terrain across the Murcia region, characterized by its rugged landscapes and extensive trail networks. The area features the intricate Guadalentín Valley, the mountainous Sierra de la Almenara, and the coastal stretches of Parque Regional Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre. Cyclists can navigate through a mix of valley paths, limestone mountains, and coastal trails, providing varied environments for all skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Lorca The most popular mountain bike trail is Coy, Albergue Coto Real – Ruta Circular – Sierra de Burete, a 26.5 miles (42.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 11 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Sierra de Burete mountains. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cave of Wolves – Bolnuevo Sandstone Formations loop from Ramonete, a moderate 34.9 miles (56.2 km) path. This route offers coastal views and passes by unique sandstone formations. Local mountain bikers also love the Guevara Palace – Puentes Reservoir and Dam loop from Lorca San Diego, a 23.9 miles (38.4 km) trail leading through the local countryside and past the Puentes Reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Mountain biking around Lorca is defined by its varied terrain, including the Guadalentín Valley, the Sierra de la Almenara, and the coastal paths of Cabo Cope. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Lorca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from nearly 100 reviews. More than 1,000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Lorca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Lorca?
A: Lorca offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 120 routes available. These trails cater to various skill levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Lorca?
A: The region around Lorca boasts diverse terrain, including the intricate Guadalentín Valley, the mountainous Sierra de la Almenara, and coastal stretches of Parque Regional Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre. You'll encounter a mix of valley paths, limestone mountains, and coastal trails, often passing through olive and almond groves, farmlands, and rugged natural landscapes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Lorca?
A: Yes, Lorca has options for beginners. While many routes are moderate, there are at least 3 trails classified as easy. For a relaxed ride, consider the 14-kilometer Bolnuevo—Puntas de Calnegre Trail mentioned in the region research, which is ideal for a more relaxed outing.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge can explore routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e920428268/coy-albergue-coto-real-ruta-circular-sierra-de-burete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coy, Albergue Coto Real – Ruta Circular – Sierra de Burete&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 42.7 km trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33787896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountainbike loop from Lorca San Diego&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 53.9 km and features over 1400 meters of ascent.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Lorca?
A: The best times to enjoy mountain biking in Lorca are during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities. While summer can be hot, coastal routes or early morning/late afternoon rides can still be enjoyable.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural attractions to see while mountain biking?
A: Absolutely! Many trails offer stunning views. You can ride past unique geological formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/998557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolnuevo Sandstone Formations&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40379854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of Wolves – Bolnuevo Sandstone Formations loop from Ramonete&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3141198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cocón de Cope&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint and the rugged coastline of Parque Regional Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Lorca?
A: Yes, Lorca offers several circular mountain bike routes. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e920428268/coy-albergue-coto-real-ruta-circular-sierra-de-burete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coy, Albergue Coto Real – Ruta Circular – Sierra de Burete&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38243666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guevara Palace – Puentes Reservoir and Dam loop from Lorca San Diego&lt;/a&gt;. These loops allow you to start and finish at the same point, making logistics easier.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Lorca?
A: The mountain bike routes in Lorca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from nearly 100 reviews. Over 1,000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Lorca's varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any coastal mountain biking options near Lorca?
A: Yes, the Parque Regional Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre offers 17 kilometers of unspoiled coastline with trails suitable for mountain biking. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40379854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of Wolves – Bolnuevo Sandstone Formations loop from Ramonete&lt;/a&gt; provide coastal views and a unique riding experience.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails around Lorca?
A: While specific regulations can vary by trail and protected area, generally, dogs are permitted on many outdoor trails in Spain, often on a leash. It's always best to check local signage or specific park rules for the trail you plan to ride to ensure it's dog-friendly.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks I can visit while mountain biking in Lorca?
A: Lorca is rich in history. You can find trails that pass by or near historical sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38243666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guevara Palace – Puentes Reservoir and Dam loop from Lorca San Diego&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the local countryside and past the Puentes Reservoir. The region also features impressive structures like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6012789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lorca Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/535410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cope Tower&lt;/a&gt;, which can be points of interest on your rides.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Lorca?
A: Many trailheads around Lorca, especially those starting from towns or popular natural areas, offer designated parking. For routes originating from Lorca San Diego, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10320817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carretera de la Parroquia – Aqueduct of the 17 Arches loop&lt;/a&gt;, you can typically find parking within the town or at the start of the trail. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for parking recommendations.
tips: The old forest refuge of Coto Real (Cehegín) has been rehabilitated and is now a bioclimatic building built with, in part, native wood and marble from the area. The old Arab roof tiles of the original shelter have been preserved. This refuge is located within a protection zone for birds (among them the booted eagle, the short-toed eagle, the eagle owl, the goshawk and the sparrow hawk) and the electricity supply has been carried out through an underground line so as not to harm these protected bird species. The refuge is located in the most mountainous area of the region and is surrounded by forests of Aleppo pine and Kermes oak. You will also find rosemary, juniper, rockrose, thyme, esparto grass and other plant species. In spring, you may be lucky enough to spot up to ten species of orchids, some of them endemic to this area.
Excellent narrow path that goes to the Coto Real Refuge between pines.
